info.title: deployment-service internal API (consumed contract)
info.description: Internal API surface of the external deployment-service, as consumed by anvilkit-export-worker. Request/response shapes preserve PRD 0008 §8.2 / PRD 0010 §8.2 exactly — this document is the cross-service contract, not an invention of this repo. deployment-service is external to anvilkit-platform: it is never implemented here; a contract-conformant mock (FR-022) encodes these shapes plus the BD-004 interim semantics (ADR-004: idempotent accept of an identical pointer re-POST). All calls carry Authorization: Bearer <INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN> (§11.1, BD-002/ADR-002). Timeouts/5xx map to retryable DEPLOYMENT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT; auth failures classify non-retryable DEPLOYMENT_SERVICE_401 / DEPLOYMENT_SERVICE_403 with ops alerts (PRD 0010 §13).

servers.description: Internal cluster address (mock deployment-service in local compose)

get.description: The record — not the event — drives the job (FR-004). Event hints (renderMode/version/slug) are reconciled against this record; a mismatch is non-retryable VALIDATION_FAILED.

401.description: Service identity rejected — worker classifies DEPLOYMENT_SERVICE_401 (non-retryable, ops alert)

403.description: Caller refused — worker classifies DEPLOYMENT_SERVICE_403 (non-retryable, ops alert)

patch.description: All status writes are CAS (from → to). The worker sets only EXPORTING, ARTIFACT_READY, EXPORT_FAILED (FR-006). On 409 STATUS_CONFLICT the worker stops safely and acks only when the deployment is terminal/non-actionable. Success response body is unspecified by the source contract; 204 No Content is the Recommended Approach (To Be Confirmed with the deployment-service owner alongside BD-004).

409.description: CAS mismatch — another actor advanced the deployment; worker stops safely (STATUS_CONFLICT, PRD 0010 §13)

post.description: Keyed by deploymentId: one deploymentId → at most one artifact manifest (FR-012, FR-015). deployment-service treats the manifest in storage as canonical and stores this pointer/summary alongside it. Repeat submission with an identical pointer must be accepted or answered with a benign conflict — BD-004, To Be Confirmed with the deployment-service owner; the interim mock semantics are idempotent accept (ADR-004).

204.description: Pointer accepted (including idempotent accept of an identical re-POST, per the ADR-004 interim default)

409.description: Reserved for the BD-004 benign-conflict branch (non-identical re-POST, or identical re-POST if BD-004 resolves to benign conflict). Shape To Be Confirmed; not generated into clients until BD-004 is resolved.

deploymentId.name: deploymentId
deploymentId.description: Deployment record ID — the canonical idempotency key

internalServiceToken.description: INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN — internal service-to-service bearer token (PRD 0010 §11.1, ADR-002). Dual-token acceptance during rotation.

DeploymentRecord.description: Authoritative deployment record (read shape of PRD 0008 §8.2). The deployment-service owns this data; the worker never reads its database directly.

DeploymentStatus.description: Full DeploymentStatus enum owned by deployment-service (PRD 0010 §6.3). The worker sets only EXPORTING, ARTIFACT_READY, EXPORT_FAILED via CAS.

StatusUpdateRequest.description: CAS status transition body (PRD 0008 §8.2 with reason/traceId per report 0010 §10.2). Every transition is audited: deployment-service writes deployment_events.

StatusConflictError.description: 409 body on CAS mismatch (PRD 0008 §8.2). The worker stops safely; ack only when the deployment is terminal/non-actionable.

ArtifactPointer.description: Artifact manifest pointer/summary (PRD 0008 §8.2, manifestDigest/entry/routes per D-3). Unlike the deployment.artifact.ready event (ADR-001), this submission DOES carry routes[] — always an array.

artifactBasePath.description: Storage base path, confined to sites/{siteId}/deployments/{deploymentId} (FR-010)

routes.description: Route table — always an array (FR-012 invariant)

ArtifactRoute.description: One route mapping within the artifact
